title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: CatLLM ecosystem R packages use large language models (LLMs) categorize open-ended text — survey responses, social media posts, academic papers, policy documents, web content — scale. ’s designed researchers want quantitative analysis free-text data without manual coding hiring research assistants. CatLLM achieves 98% accuracy compared human consensus classification tasks using leading models GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Qwen 3. Validated expert human coders across 21 LLMs 4 surveys; see SocArXiv preprint methodology. R packages thin reticulate wrappers around underlying Python implementation. Every parameter, default, behavior identical Python version — calling syntax differs. deep conceptual content, advanced configuration, full 50-parameter classify() reference, Python cat-llm README canonical source.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Install meta-package (brings 7 sub-packages) R-universe: install single domain package lighter footprint: One-time setup: install Python backend (requires Python 3.9+ system):
code: install.packages(   "cat.llm",   repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",             "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) install.packages(   c("cat.stack", "cat.survey"),   repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",             "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) library(cat.llm) install_cat_stack()  # With PDF processing support: # install_cat_stack(pdf = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: CatLLM designed building datasets scale, one-queries. can classify individual responses, primary purpose batch processing entire text columns, image collections, PDF corpora structured research datasets. outputs R data.frames ready analysis CSV export.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: library(cat.llm) attaches every domain package exposes domain-suffixed aliases (classify_survey(), classify_political(), classify_social(), etc.):
code: library(cat.llm)  api_key <- Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")  # Domain-neutral classification (from cat.stack) results <- classify(   input_data  = c("I love this product!", "Terrible experience.", "It was fine."),   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   description = "Customer feedback sentiment",   api_key     = api_key )  # Survey classification — adds survey-tuned prompts results <- classify_survey(   input_data      = df$responses,   categories      = c("Job change", "Family reasons", "Cost of living"),   survey_question = "Why did you move to a new city?",   api_key         = api_key )  # Academic paper classification — fetches by journal results <- classify_academic(   input_data    = NULL,   categories    = c("Empirical", "Theoretical", "Review"),   journal_issn  = "0894-4393",   paper_limit   = 50L,   polite_email  = "you@university.edu",   api_key       = api_key )  # Social media classification results <- classify_social(   input_data = df$posts,   categories = c("Misinformation", "Opinion", "News"),   api_key    = api_key )  # Political text classification (built-in registered sources) results <- classify_political(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   since      = "2025-01-01",   n          = 50L,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance"),   api_key    = api_key )  # Cognitive assessment scoring (CERAD drawings) scores <- cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "diamond",   image_input = df$drawing_paths,   api_key     = api_key )

what: Option B — install only the domain you need
title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: lighter dependency footprint, install package actually use: two options produce identical results — classify_survey() cat.llm just thin re-export cat.survey::classify().
code: # install.packages("cat.survey", repos = ...) library(cat.survey)  results <- cat.survey::classify(   input_data      = df$responses,   categories      = c("Job change", "Family reasons", "Cost of living"),   survey_question = "Why did you move to a new city?",   api_key         = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Every domain package shares core API — classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize() (applicable) — depends cat.stack, holds underlying classification engine.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: recommendations based empirical testing across 4 surveys, 4 models (7B frontier-class), 250-row subsamples compared human-coded ground truth. apply identically R Python.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Detailed category descriptions — single biggest lever accuracy. Instead short labels like "Job change", use verbose descriptions like "person job school career change, including transferred retired." Consistently improves accuracy several percentage points across models. Include “” category — catch-like ": response fit categories." prevents model forcing ambiguous responses ill-fitting categories. default, R wrappers prompt add one category list lacks one (add_other = "prompt"). -shot examples (example1–example6) — providing 2-4 labeled examples can help, especially weaker models. Effects modest (+0–1 pp average) model-dependent. Low temperature (creativity = 0) — classification, deterministic output preferable. Higher temperatures add noise without improving accuracy.
code: verbose_categories <- c(   "Job/school: A change in employment, education, or career, including transfers and retirement.",   "Family: Relationship changes, having children, supporting relatives, or relocating to be near family.",   "Cost of living: Housing affordability, cost of goods, or general economic pressure.",   "Other: The response does not fit any of the above categories." )  results <- classify(   input_data = df$responses,   categories = verbose_categories,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) results <- classify(   input_data = df$responses,   categories = verbose_categories,   example1   = list(text = "Took a new job in Chicago", label = "Job/school"),   example2   = list(text = "Wanted to be closer to grandkids", label = "Family"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Chain Thought (chain_of_thought = TRUE): measurable improvement testing; slightly degraded performance models. default. Chain Verification (chain_of_verification = TRUE): uses ~4x API calls self-verification. Consistently reduced accuracy 1–2 pp retracting correct classifications. recommended classification. Step-back prompting (step_back_prompt = TRUE): inconsistent — slight gains weaker models (~+1.8 pp), slight losses stronger ones (~−0.5 pp). recommended default. Context prompting (context_prompt = TRUE): consistent benefit observed.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: effective approach straightforward: write detailed category descriptions, include “” category, use capable model low temperature. Advanced prompting adds complexity cost without reliable gains classification.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Get API key preferred provider: providers require adding payment method. Store key securely never share publicly.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Rather pasting key scripts, store ~/.Renviron ’s automatically available every R session: restart R use: find edit .Renviron: saving, restart R changes take effect.
code: OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... GOOGLE_API_KEY=AIza... api_key <- Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") usethis::edit_r_environ()   # opens it for editing; creates if missing

what: Run entirely locally with no API key
title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: sensitive data, free use, air-gapped environments, run local model via Ollama: ⚠️ Disk-space heads-: qwen2.5:14b ~9 GB disk Ollama needs roughly much free download. Check df -h / first — ~12 GB free, use qwen2.5:7b. API key needed; data never leaves machine.
code: # In a separate terminal: install Ollama, then pull a model. # Recommended (larger, more accurate, ~9 GB): #   ollama pull qwen2.5:14b # Smaller fallback if disk/RAM constrained (~4.7 GB): #   ollama pull qwen2.5:7b  results <- classify(   input_data    = df$responses,   categories    = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   user_model    = "qwen2.5:14b",   # or "qwen2.5:7b" if you pulled the smaller one   model_source  = "ollama" )

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Specify via user_model = "...": OpenAI: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4, gpt-5, … Anthropic: claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, claude-3-5-haiku-20241022, … Google: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro, … HuggingFace: Qwen/Qwen3-235B, meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3, thousands community models xAI: grok-2, … Mistral: mistral-large-latest, pixtral-large-latest, … Perplexity: sonar-large, sonar-small, … Ollama (local): qwen2.5:14b (recommended, ~9 GB), qwen2.5:7b (smaller fallback, ~4.7 GB), llama3.1:8b, … (set model_source = "ollama") Fully tested: OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, Google Gemini (free tier 5 RPM limit), HuggingFace, xAI, Mistral. best results starting , OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini) Anthropic (claude-3-5-haiku-20241022) cheap, fast, reliable.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Run input multiple models combine results via majority voting. Often improves accuracy reducing individual model biases. output data.frame includes per-model predictions (e.g., category_1_gpt_4o_mini, category_1_claude) plus consensus column.
code: results <- classify(   input_data = df$responses,   categories = verbose_categories,   models = list(     c("gpt-4o-mini",            "openai",    Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")),     c("claude-3-5-haiku-20241022", "anthropic", Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")),     c("gemini-2.5-flash",       "google",    Sys.getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"))   ),   consensus_threshold = "unanimous"   # or 0.5 for majority, etc. )

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Every parameter Python classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize() functions exposed R identical semantics. full per-parameter documentation lives -R help system R-universe per-package reference manuals. full conceptual coverage every parameter — batch mode, prompt tuning, embeddings, JSON formatting, advanced ensemble configurations — see Python README API Reference. R wrappers expose every Python kwarg.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: adapting Python examples project README, table covers syntax differences. conversions handled automatically reticulate::r_to_py() inside R wrappers — write R, wrapper passes Python.

title: Getting Started with CatLLM for R
text: Full conceptual reference: Python cat-llm README — covers every parameter, advanced configuration, prompt tuning, embeddings, etc. Since R thin reticulate layer, every Python concept applies directly. Per-package R reference manuals: https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev — pick package, click “Reference Manual” link full @param docs. End--end smoke test: see r-package/test--packages.R GitHub repo — single R script installs 8 packages runs minimal classification per package. Issues, questions, contributions: github.com/chrissoria/cat-llm/issues Citation — use CatLLM published research, please cite: Soria, C. (2026). Scaling Open-Ended Survey Coding: LLM Pipeline Definitions Heavy Lifting. SocArXiv. https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/gjvcf_v1 software DOI: Soria, C. (2026). CatLLM: Reproducible Python Ecosystem Generating, Assigning, Scoring Open-Ended Text, Images, Documents Across Research Domains (v3.0.0) [Software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19960067

what: cat.llm — The CatLLM R ecosystem meta-package
title: cat.llm — The CatLLM R ecosystem meta-package
text: cat.llm meta-package CatLLM R ecosystem — tidyverse LLM-powered text classification social science. Installing cat.llm brings full family domain-specific packages.

title: cat.llm — The CatLLM R ecosystem meta-package
text: install Python backend (one-time):
code: # Install the meta-package from R-universe (brings everything) install.packages("cat.llm",                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # Or install individual packages install.packages(c("cat.stack", "cat.survey", "cat.vader",                    "cat.ademic", "cat.cog", "cat.pol", "cat.web"),                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org")) library(cat.llm) install_cat_stack()

title: cat.llm — The CatLLM R ecosystem meta-package
code: library(cat.llm) # -- Attaching cat.llm ecosystem -- # v 0.1.0 cat.stack # v 0.1.0 cat.survey # v 0.1.0 cat.vader # v 0.1.0 cat.ademic # v 0.1.0 cat.cog # v 0.1.0 cat.pol # v 0.1.0 cat.web  # Base classification (from cat.stack) results <- classify(   input_data = c("Great product!", "Terrible experience."),   categories = c("Positive", "Negative"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Survey classification (domain-suffixed alias) results <- classify_survey(   input_data      = df$responses,   categories      = c("Satisfied", "Dissatisfied", "Neutral"),   survey_question = "How satisfied are you with our service?",   api_key         = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Social media classification results <- classify_social(   sm_source = "reddit",   sm_handle = "technology",   sm_limit  = 100,   categories = c("Opinion", "News", "Question"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Or use domain packages directly library(cat.survey) cat.survey::classify(...)

title: cat.llm — The CatLLM R ecosystem meta-package

title: Domain-suffixed aliases for the CatLLM ecosystem — catllm-aliases
text: ... Additional arguments passed Python function.

title: Domain-suffixed aliases for the CatLLM ecosystem — catllm-aliases
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(cat.llm)  # Survey classification (re-export of cat.survey::classify) classify_survey(   input_data      = df$responses,   categories      = c("Cost", "Quality", "Service", "Other"),   survey_question = "Why did you choose us?",   api_key         = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Political documents (re-export of cat.pol::classify) classify_political(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   n          = 50L,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Web content (re-export of cat.web::classify) classify_web(   input_data    = c("https://example.com/article-1",                     "https://example.com/article-2"),   categories    = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial"),   source_domain = "example.com",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # CERAD cognitive scoring (re-export of cat.cog::cerad_drawn_score) cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "circle",   image_input = "./drawings/",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) } # }

what: Attach all CatLLM ecosystem packages — catllm_attach

text: Explicitly loads domain packages. Normally happens automatically library(cat.llm) called, function can used reload detaching.

title: Attach all CatLLM ecosystem packages — catllm_attach
Attach all CatLLM ecosystem packages — catllm_attach
text: Invisibly returns character vector attached package names.

title: Attach all CatLLM ecosystem packages — catllm_attach
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Normally this happens automatically on `library(cat.llm)`. # Call manually to re-attach after detaching: catllm_attach() } # }

title: Re-exported functions from cat.stack — reexports
text: cat.llm meta-package re-exports core functions cat.stack available immediately library(cat.llm). Behavior arguments identical originals — links point canonical documentation cat.stack.

title: Re-exported functions from cat.stack — reexports
text: classify Classify text, images, PDFs predefined categories. extract Discover extract categories data scheme predefined. explore Raw category extraction saturation analysis. summarize Summarize text, images, PDFs several formats. prompt_tune Interactive, human---loop tuning classification system prompt. install_cat_stack One-time installer Python catstack backend. ensure_ollama_running Check (optionally start) local Ollama server running local-model classification. list_ollama_models List locally installed Ollama models. check_ollama_model Check whether specific Ollama model installed locally. pull_ollama_model Download Ollama model local installation.

title: cat.llm 3.1.0
text: Added cat.pol cat.web meta-package; full ecosystem now 7 sub-packages (cat.stack base + 6 domain packages). New domain-suffixed aliases: classify_political(), extract_political(), explore_political(), summarize_political(), classify_web(), extract_web(), explore_web(), summarize_web(). Startup message now lists 7 sub-packages.

title: cat.llm 3.0.0
text: Initial meta-package release. Installs loads full CatLLM ecosystem (cat.stack, cat.survey, cat.vader, cat.ademic, cat.cog). Provides domain-suffixed aliases (classify_survey(), classify_social(), classify_academic(), cerad_drawn_score()) re-exported sub-package.
